title: Centrolepis pusilla
taxonomy: Centrolepis pusilla is classified as a species of plant in the genus Centrolepis and family Centrolepidaceae. It belongs to the order Poales and subclass Commelinidae. This species is a type of sedge that is endemic to New Zealand. The Centrolepidaceae family is a small family of flowering plants that is mostly found in the southern hemisphere. Centrolepis pusilla is a unique and relatively unknown plant species.

intro: Centrolepis pusilla, also known as dwarf club-rush, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Centrolepidaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Centrolepis pusilla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
